categories.label: Earnings & Financial Results
categories.description: Quarterly earnings reports, revenue/EPS figures, profit margins, guidance, dividends, capex forecasts, stock buybacks, and any article reporting specific financial results versus expectations.

categories.label: Market & Sector Sentiment
categories.description: Broad market roundups, sector rotation, macro data mentions, index movements, and multi-stock previews where META is mentioned alongside peers rather than as the primary subject.

categories.label: Analyst & Consensus Signals
categories.description: Analyst upgrades, downgrades, price target changes, buy/sell ratings, and institutional positioning commentary on META specifically.

categories.label: AI Strategy & Infrastructure
categories.description: Development and release of AI models (Llama, Movie Gen, Meta AI assistant), AI infrastructure investments, custom chips, data center buildouts, energy deals, open-source AI strategy, and AI-powered product features.

categories.label: Regulatory, Antitrust & Compliance
categories.description: EU DMA/DSA proceedings, antitrust fines, FTC actions, competition probes, data privacy rulings, GDPR enforcement, government investigations, and regulatory actions targeting Meta's business practices across jurisdictions.

categories.label: Child Safety & Content Moderation
categories.description: Lawsuits and legislation targeting youth social media use, teen account protections, sextortion enforcement, content moderation policies, Senate hearings on child exploitation, and platform safety features for minors.

categories.label: Hardware, AR/VR & Metaverse
categories.description: Quest headsets, Orion AR glasses, Reality Labs initiatives, EssilorLuxottica smart eyewear partnership, mixed reality ecosystem, and hardware manufacturing strategy.

categories.label: Platform & Product Updates
categories.description: Feature launches across Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, Threads, and Messenger including creator tools, business integrations, advertising capabilities, music deals, service outages, and product updates.

categories.label: Geopolitical & Government Relations
categories.description: Country-specific bans and access restrictions (Turkey, Russia, Brazil), government content removal requests, election integrity measures, foreign interference takedowns, political content decisions, and international policy disputes.

dimensions.scale: 1=routine operational update, minor product tweak, or corporate filing with no revenue/margin implications, 10=event directly and substantially impacts revenue streams, cost structure, or total addressable market
1=outcome fully expected or information already widely known, 10=completely unexpected revelation that contradicts prior consensus or assumptions
1=transient event affecting only the next few days, 10=structural shift with implications lasting years
1=neutral or balanced tone with no clear positive/negative lean, 10=extremely strong positive or negative tone throughout the article
1=pure opinion, speculation, or vague commentary with no specific data, 10=rich in hard data, specific figures, named sources, and quantified outcomes
1=ambiguous implications that could reasonably be interpreted as positive or negative, 10=unambiguously good or bad for the company with no reasonable counter-interpretation
1=affects only this company specifically, 5=sector-wide implications, 10=broad macro or market-wide event
1=no regulatory, legal, or compliance dimension, 10=major legal ruling, regulatory fine, or compliance mandate with direct financial or operational consequences
1=no effect on competitive positioning, 10=major shift in competitive dynamics through partnerships, market entry, or rival disruption
1=no leadership involvement or strategic implication, 10=major CEO/executive-level strategic announcement, organizational restructuring, or leadership change signaling new direction
1=reinforces the prevailing investment thesis and existing narrative about the company, 10=fundamentally challenges or redirects the market's understanding of the company's trajectory
1=rehash or follow-up of previously reported information with no new facts, 10=first report of genuinely new information not previously in the public domain
1=no financial results reported or results exactly match expectations, 10=reported figures dramatically exceed or miss prior guidance and analyst expectations as described in the article
1=transient story that resolves or fades within a day, 10=persistent story that remains relevant and develops over weeks or months

kept.materiality: Consensus 3/3. Ranges from Form 4 filings and minor feature announcements to $1.4B settlements and quarterly earnings with major capex guidance — wide variation across the corpus.
kept.surprise: Consensus 3/3. Spans from scheduled product launches and routine regulatory actions to the first-ever dividend declaration, 16% share price plunge on unexpected capex guidance, and $1.4B Texas settlement — strong variation in how unexpected outcomes were.
kept.temporal_horizon: Consensus 3/3. Ranges from one-day service outages to structural shifts like the EU DMA compliance model, open-source AI strategy, and Orion AR glasses prototype revealing a decade-long hardware strategy.
kept.sentiment_strength: Consensus 3/3. Varies from neutral corporate blog posts and Form 4 filings to strongly negative articles about child exploitation hearings and strongly positive articles about tripled earnings and dividend initiation.
kept.information_density: Consensus 3/3. Ranges from one-line headline fragments and opinion pieces to detailed earnings transcripts with specific revenue, EPS, margin figures, and analyst price targets.
kept.directional_clarity: Consensus 3/3. Some articles are clearly positive (earnings beat, dividend) or negative (antitrust fines, settlement), while others like AI spending increases are genuinely ambiguous across time horizons.
kept.scope: Consensus 3/3. Ranges from META-only events like Threads feature updates to sector-wide Big Tech regulatory crackdowns and broad macro articles about market selloffs.
kept.regulatory_risk: Consensus 3/3. Highly variable — from zero regulatory content in product announcements to major EU DMA charges, $1.4B Texas settlement, FTC privacy actions, and multiple country-level investigations.
kept.competitive_impact: Consensus 3/3. Ranges from no competitive angle in routine updates to major competitive moves like open-sourcing Llama 3, EssilorLuxottica partnership, and Movie Gen rivaling OpenAI's Sora.
kept.management_signal: Consensus 2/3 (kept runs 1, 3; run 2 dropped in favor of custom strategic_signal). Ranges from no leadership angle in routine news to Zuckerberg's Senate apology, AI team reorganization, open-source AI vision letters, and Reality Labs restructuring.
kept.narrative_shift: Consensus 3/3. Most articles reinforce existing narratives, but some shift the thesis — first dividend signals capital return era, Q1 capex guidance sparks AI spending fears, Orion prototype reframes hardware ambitions.
kept.repeatedness: Consensus 3/3. Clear variation between first-break stories (dividend announcement, Orion reveal, Texas settlement) and repeated multi-outlet coverage of the same events or follow-up articles days later.
kept.financial_result_surprise: Consensus 3/3. Most articles contain no financial results, but earnings articles show clear variation — Q4 2023 EPS tripled expectations while Q1 2024 guidance disappointed and shares plunged 16%.
kept.expected_duration: Consensus 2/3 (kept runs 1, 3; run 2 dropped as redundant with temporal_horizon). Retained because temporal_horizon captures how far forward the impact reaches while expected_duration captures how long the news story itself persists in coverage — a regulatory ruling may have a years-long horizon but fade from headlines in days.

dropped.actionability: Dropped by all 3 runs. Clusters at extremes — earnings and analyst actions are immediately actionable while regulatory and product articles are not — largely duplicates materiality without adding discriminating power.
dropped.controversy: Dropped by all 3 runs. Most META articles reflect consensus views rather than divisive investor debates. The few controversial items overlap heavily with directional_clarity and would score uniformly low for investor-relevant controversy.
dropped.strategic_signal: Custom dimension from run 2 only (1/1). The underlying concept overlaps substantially with management_signal which was kept by 2/3 runs. Not robust enough for consensus.
